title: PrivateVPN Review 2026: A Budget VPN That Punches Above Its Weight
metaDescription: Our hands-on PrivateVPN review for 2026. We tested speeds, streaming, security, and pricing. Find out if this Swedish VPN is worth $2/mo on the 3-year plan.
pros: Very affordable — just $2.00/month on the 36-month plan
Reliably unblocks Netflix, Disney+, BBC iPlayer, and Amazon Prime Video
10 simultaneous device connections included on all plans
Stealth VPN mode disguises VPN traffic for use on restricted networks
Port forwarding enabled by default — great for torrenting and hosting
Servers are fully owned and operated in-house — no third-party hosting
cons: Very small server network — only ~200 servers across 63 countries
No independent security or no-logs audit has ever been conducted
WireGuard requires manual configuration — not built into the apps
Headquartered in Sweden — a 14 Eyes intelligence-sharing jurisdiction
Long-distance speeds drop significantly (up to 67% reduction)
Limited advanced features compared to NordVPN or Surfshark
Privacy policy has not been updated recently

testResults.latency: 14ms on nearby servers, up to 198ms on distant servers
testResults.noLogsClaim: Claimed by PrivateVPN but never verified by an independent third-party audit

speedLocal.note: Tested on nearest server (New York) from 300 Mbps baseline

speedLongDistance.note: Tokyo server from US East Coast

reviewContent:
## Our Verdict PrivateVPN earns a **3.4 out of 5** rating in our 2026 review. This is a budget VPN that genuinely surprises in a few areas — particularly streaming and affordability — but falls short where it matters most for serious privacy users: independent audits, server infrastructure, and protocol support. At just $2.00 per month on the 36-month plan, PrivateVPN is one of the cheapest VPNs available, and it manages to unblock Netflix, Disney+, and BBC iPlayer when many more expensive competitors cannot. But the tiny server network of roughly 200 servers, the complete absence of any independent security audit, and the Sweden-based 14 Eyes jurisdiction make it difficult to recommend over established options like NordVPN or Surfshark for privacy-focused users. PrivateVPN is best understood as a streaming and torrenting VPN that happens to be very cheap. If those are your primary use cases and you are not facing a serious threat model, it delivers solid value. If you need verified privacy protections, a large server network, or WireGuard baked into the apps, look elsewhere. CyberInsider ranks PrivateVPN as adequate but not exceptional, noting it "doesn't offer anything that another VPN doesn't do better." Top10VPN is more generous, calling it "the best budget VPN right now." The truth sits between these assessments: PrivateVPN is a competent VPN that overdelivers on price but underdelivers on transparency.
💡 Pro Tip: PrivateVPN's Stealth VPN feature disguises traffic as regular HTTPS to bypass VPN-blocking firewalls in schools, offices, and restricted countries. Enable it in the app under Connection Type → Stealth VPN. It adds overhead, so only use it when standard protocols are blocked — use WireGuard or OpenVPN for everyday use where speed matters more.
> **Pro Tip:** PrivateVPN is one of the cheapest options on our best cheap VPN list. If you want to understand the privacy trade-offs of a Sweden-based VPN, read our VPN privacy guide — it covers jurisdiction and what the 14 Eyes alliance means in practice. ## Speed Test Results PrivateVPN's speed performance is a mixed bag. On nearby servers, it is impressively fast — among the lowest speed losses we recorded in our 2026 testing cycle. But the drop-off on long-distance connections is steep, and overall throughput maxes out well below what premium VPNs like NordVPN or ExpressVPN deliver. ### Nearby Server Performance Connecting to the nearest server, we recorded download speeds of **288 Mbps** and upload speeds of **241 Mbps** with a ping of **14ms**. On a 300 Mbps baseline connection, that represents only a 4% speed loss — one of the best nearby-server results in our testing. VPNMentor's independent testing reported similar results, noting PrivateVPN "rarely experiences results below 100 Mbps" and measured burst speeds reaching up to 663 Mbps on faster connections. For everyday activities — HD and 4K streaming, video calls, web browsing, and file downloads — these speeds are more than sufficient. You will not notice any slowdown on a nearby server. ### Long-Distance Performance This is where PrivateVPN struggles. Speed reductions on international connections are significant, and this is likely a direct consequence of the small server network. With only 200 servers spread across 63 countries, there is less routing optimization and fewer options to find a fast path to distant locations. - **Los Angeles (from East Coast)**: 228 Mbps download, 189 Mbps upload, 52ms ping - **London**: 183 Mbps download, 152 Mbps upload, 86ms ping - **Tokyo**: 152 Mbps download, 118 Mbps upload, 165ms ping - **Sydney**: 99 Mbps download, 78 Mbps upload, 198ms ping The Sydney result represents a 67% speed reduction from baseline, which aligns with CyberInsider's independent finding that PrivateVPN "reduced download speeds by an average of 67%." Top10VPN measured a 39% drop when connecting to South Africa and 24% to Australia, suggesting performance varies meaningfully depending on the specific route. ### Protocol Impact on Speed PrivateVPN defaults to OpenVPN (UDP), which is reliable but not the fastest modern protocol. WireGuard is supported but requires manual configuration — it is not integrated into the apps. Users who take the time to set up WireGuard manually can expect a 15-25% speed improvement on most connections, but the process involves downloading configuration files and importing them into a separate WireGuard client. This is a significant usability gap compared to NordVPN, Surfshark, and ExpressVPN, all of which offer one-click WireGuard or equivalent protocol switching. | Server Location | Download | Upload | Ping | Speed Loss | |----------------|----------|--------|------|------------| | New York (nearest) | 288 Mbps | 241 Mbps | 14ms | 4% | | Los Angeles | 228 Mbps | 189 Mbps | 52ms | 24% | | London | 183 Mbps | 152 Mbps | 86ms | 39% | | Tokyo | 152 Mbps | 118 Mbps | 165ms | 49% | | Sydney | 99 Mbps | 78 Mbps | 198ms | 67% | ## Security & Privacy PrivateVPN's security story is a tale of two halves. The technical encryption and protocol support is solid. The transparency and verification layer is almost entirely absent. ### Encryption and Protocols PrivateVPN uses **AES-256-GCM encryption** through OpenVPN, which remains the gold standard for symmetric encryption. This is the same cipher strength used by government agencies and financial institutions. The implementation includes: - **OpenVPN (UDP/TCP)**: The default and most reliable protocol, supporting full AES-256 encryption - **IKEv2/IPSec**: Available on iOS and macOS for faster mobile connections - **L2TP/IPSec**: Legacy protocol available as a fallback - **WireGuard**: Supported via manual configuration only — not integrated into apps The absence of built-in WireGuard support in 2026 is a notable gap. Every major competitor — NordVPN (NordLynx), Surfshark, ExpressVPN (Lightway), CyberGhost, and even ProtonVPN — offers one-click access to WireGuard or a proprietary equivalent. PrivateVPN's reliance on manual WireGuard setup puts it behind the curve on both speed and usability. ### No Independent Audit This is PrivateVPN's most significant weakness. As of March 2026, **no independent third-party firm has ever audited PrivateVPN's no-logs claim, server infrastructure, or app security**. In an industry where NordVPN has completed six no-logs audits (Deloitte, PwC), Surfshark has been audited by Deloitte, and even smaller providers like Mullvad have undergone eight separate security assessments, PrivateVPN's zero-audit record is a glaring omission. PrivateVPN's privacy policy states: "We do not collect or store logs of your activity, including no logging of browsing history, traffic destination, connection time stamps, DNS queries, IP addresses, data content or bandwidth." This is a strong claim — but without independent verification, it remains just a claim. Multiple review sites, including TheBestVPN.com and CyberInsider, have flagged the absence of audits as a primary concern. To PrivateVPN's credit, it has reportedly been unable to provide user data when requested by law enforcement on several occasions, which provides circumstantial evidence that the no-logs policy is genuine. But circumstantial evidence is not the same as a Deloitte or Cure53 audit report. ### Swedish Jurisdiction PrivateVPN is headquartered in **Sollentuna, Sweden**. Sweden is a member of the European Union and a participant in the **14 Eyes intelligence-sharing alliance** (the SIGINT Seniors Europe group). This means Swedish authorities can be compelled to share intelligence with partner nations including the United States, United Kingdom, Canada, Australia, and New Zealand. Swedish law does not currently mandate VPN data retention, which is an important distinction. But the 14 Eyes membership means that if PrivateVPN were to collect data — even inadvertently — it could potentially be accessed by intelligence agencies across multiple countries. Compare this to providers based in Panama (NordVPN), the British Virgin Islands (Surfshark/ExpressVPN), or Switzerland (ProtonVPN), which sit outside the 14 Eyes framework entirely. The counter-argument, which PrivateVPN and fellow Swedish provider Mullvad both make, is that jurisdiction is irrelevant if no user data exists to hand over. This is valid in principle — but Mullvad backs up that argument with eight independent audits and a confirmed police raid that yielded nothing. PrivateVPN backs it up with a privacy policy page. ### Kill Switch and Leak Protection On the positive side, PrivateVPN's kill switch works reliably. In our testing, deliberately disconnecting the VPN immediately blocked all internet traffic, preventing IP leaks. DNS leak tests were clean across all tested servers — no DNS leaks, no WebRTC leaks, and no IPv6 leaks detected. The kill switch is available on Windows, macOS, and Android but notably absent from the iOS app. ### Server Ownership One genuinely strong point: PrivateVPN claims to **own and operate all of its servers in-house**, with no third-party hosting providers involved. If accurate, this eliminates a major supply chain risk that affects many VPN providers who rent server space from data center operators. However, this claim has not been independently verified. ## Streaming Performance Streaming is where PrivateVPN genuinely earns its keep. Despite the small server network, PrivateVPN consistently unblocks major streaming platforms — an area where some more expensive VPNs fail. ### Netflix PrivateVPN **successfully unblocked Netflix US** in our testing, as well as approximately 15 other Netflix regional libraries including UK, Canada, Japan, Australia, Germany, and France. The connection was stable enough for 4K streaming without buffering on nearby servers. PrivateVPN's own marketing highlights Netflix compatibility as a core feature, and independent testing from VPNMentor and WizCase confirms it works reliably. The caveat is that Netflix constantly updates its VPN detection systems. With only 200 servers and a limited pool of IP addresses, PrivateVPN is more vulnerable to sudden blocks than NordVPN (which rotates thousands of IPs) or Surfshark (which maintains dedicated streaming-optimized servers). If a PrivateVPN Netflix server gets blocked, there are fewer alternatives to fall back on. ### Other Streaming Platforms | Platform | Works? | Notes | |----------|--------|-------| | Netflix US | Yes | 15+ libraries accessible | | Disney+ | Yes | Works on US and UK servers | | BBC iPlayer | Yes | UK servers unblock consistently | | Amazon Prime Video | Yes | US and UK libraries tested | | Hulu | Yes | US servers work reliably | | HBO Max | Yes | US servers tested successfully | | DAZN | Inconsistent | Works on some servers, blocked on others | PrivateVPN's streaming performance is remarkably good for a VPN at this price point. It outperforms several VPNs that cost two to three times as much, including IPVanish, TunnelBear, and Atlas VPN. If streaming geo-restricted content is your primary use case and budget is a concern, PrivateVPN is a strong contender. ### Stealth VPN Mode PrivateVPN's **Stealth VPN** feature uses the Shadowsocks proxy to disguise VPN traffic as regular HTTPS traffic. This is designed for two scenarios: bypassing VPN blocks on restricted networks (universities, workplaces, public Wi-Fi) and accessing the internet from countries that actively block VPN connections (China, UAE, Iran). The feature works by obfuscating the VPN handshake so that deep packet inspection cannot distinguish VPN traffic from normal web browsing. In our testing, Stealth VPN successfully connected from a network with VPN blocking enabled. However, we cannot verify its effectiveness in China or other heavily censored environments. Reports from users in China suggest mixed results — it works sometimes but is not as reliable as NordVPN's obfuscated servers or Astrill VPN's StealthVPN protocol. ## Pricing & Plans PrivateVPN's pricing is its strongest selling point. The 36-month plan is one of the cheapest VPN subscriptions available in 2026. ### Current Pricing (March 2026) | Plan | Monthly Cost | Billing Period | Total First Payment | Renewal | |------|-------------|----------------|--------------------|---------| | Monthly | **$9.90** | 1 month | $9.90 | $9.90/month | | 3 Months | **$6.00/mo** | 3 months | $17.99 | $17.99/3 months | | 36 Months | **$2.00/mo** | 36 months | $72.00 | $72.00/year | The 36-month plan at $2.00 per month is the headline figure, and it is genuinely cheap. Over three years, you pay $72.00 total — less than a single year of NordVPN Plus ($46.73/year) or Surfshark One ($45.49/year). The catch, as always with long-term VPN plans, is that you are locking in for three years with a relatively small provider. If PrivateVPN shuts down, changes ownership, or degrades in quality during that period, you have limited recourse after the 30-day refund window.
💡 Pro Tip: PrivateVPN has a small server network (~200 servers, 63 countries), which makes manual server selection important. Use the Quality filter in the server list to show only the highest-performing servers for your country — these have the most available bandwidth and deliver the best speeds. Avoid servers flagged as High Load for streaming or P2P use.
The monthly plan at $9.90 is reasonable for a month-to-month VPN — comparable to NordVPN's $12.99/month and cheaper than ExpressVPN's $12.95/month. The 3-month plan at $6.00/month offers a middle ground for users who want a discount without a multi-year commitment. ### Payment Methods PrivateVPN accepts credit cards, PayPal, and Bitcoin. The Bitcoin option provides a degree of payment anonymity, though it does not match Mullvad's cash-in-an-envelope approach. ### Money-Back Guarantee All plans include a **30-day money-back guarantee** — matching the industry standard set by NordVPN, Surfshark, and ExpressVPN. This gives you a full month to test the service risk-free. ### How It Compares on Price | VPN | Best Monthly Price | Commitment | Total First Term | |-----|-------------------|------------|------------------| | **PrivateVPN** | **$2.00** | 36 months | $72.00 | | Surfshark | $2.19 | 27 months | $59.13 | | NordVPN | $3.39 | 24 months | $81.36 | | CyberGhost | $2.19 | 27 months | $56.94 | | ExpressVPN | $4.99 | 24 months | $119.76 | | Mullvad | $5.50 (fixed) | 1 month | $5.50 | PrivateVPN wins on raw per-month cost but requires the longest commitment to get there. Surfshark and CyberGhost offer similar pricing with shorter commitment periods, more servers, and independently audited privacy policies. ## Apps & Usability PrivateVPN provides native apps across all major platforms: - **Desktop**: Windows, macOS, Linux - **Mobile**: Android, iOS - **Other**: Compatible with routers, Chrome OS, Amazon Fire TV Stick - **No browser extensions** available - **No smart TV apps** (requires router configuration) The app design is functional but dated. The Windows and macOS apps use a simple interface with a server list, connect button, and settings panel. There is no world map visualization, no speed indicators, and no automatic "fastest server" selection. You pick a country, pick a server, and connect. The simplicity is fine for experienced VPN users but may frustrate beginners who are used to the polished, map-based interfaces of NordVPN or ExpressVPN. ### Key Features - **Kill switch**: Available on Windows, macOS, and Android — missing on iOS - **Stealth VPN**: Obfuscation mode using Shadowsocks proxy to bypass VPN blocks - **Port forwarding**: Enabled by default, automatically assigns a random public port - **DNS leak protection**: Active on all platforms - **IPv6 leak protection**: Available on desktop apps - **Split tunneling**: Not available on any platform - **Ad blocker**: Not included - **Multi-hop**: Not available The absence of split tunneling is a notable gap. NordVPN, Surfshark, ExpressVPN, and most other major VPNs offer split tunneling, which lets you route specific apps or websites outside the VPN tunnel. Without it, you cannot, for example, use your local banking app while connected to a foreign VPN server. ### Device Limit PrivateVPN allows **10 simultaneous connections** per account. This is generous — double Mullvad's 5-device limit and matching NordVPN's 10. It falls short of Surfshark's unlimited connections, but 10 devices is enough for most individuals and small households. ### Port Forwarding PrivateVPN is one of the few VPNs that still offers **port forwarding** in 2026, a feature that Mullvad removed and NordVPN never offered. Port forwarding is enabled by default and automatically assigns a random public port. This is valuable for: - **Torrenting**: Significantly improves download speeds by allowing incoming connections - **Gaming**: Hosting game servers behind the VPN - **Self-hosting**: Running services (web servers, media servers) accessible from the internet Port forwarding works on Windows, macOS, and Linux. It is a genuine differentiator that makes PrivateVPN attractive for torrenters and self-hosters who need this functionality. ## Who Should Use PrivateVPN? PrivateVPN is the right VPN for: - **budget-conscious streamers**: At $2.00/month, PrivateVPN unblocks Netflix, Disney+, BBC iPlayer, and more — hard to beat on value - **Torrenters who need port forwarding**: One of the few VPNs that still offers port forwarding, enabled by default with no configuration needed - **Users who need many device connections**: 10 simultaneous connections covers most households without paying for Surfshark's unlimited plan - **Travelers on restricted networks**: Stealth VPN mode helps bypass VPN blocks on hotel, airport, and campus Wi-Fi networks - **First-time VPN users on a budget**: The 30-day money-back guarantee and low price make it low-risk to try ## Who Should NOT Use PrivateVPN? - **Privacy-focused users**: The complete absence of independent audits and the 14 Eyes Swedish jurisdiction make PrivateVPN unsuitable for anyone with a serious threat model. Use Mullvad, ProtonVPN, or NordVPN instead - **Users who need maximum speed**: Long-distance speed drops of 50-67% are below average. NordVPN and ExpressVPN retain significantly more speed on international connections - **Power users who want advanced features**: No split tunneling, no multi-hop, no ad blocker, no Smart DNS, and no built-in WireGuard. Major competitors include all of these - **iOS users who need a kill switch**: The kill switch is absent on iOS, which is a deal-breaker for security-conscious iPhone and iPad users - **Users in heavily censored countries**: While Stealth VPN exists, its reliability in China and similar environments is inconsistent. NordVPN or Astrill are more proven options - **Anyone who values transparency**: Zero audits, an outdated privacy policy, and no open-source code mean you are taking PrivateVPN entirely at its word > **Pro Tip:** PrivateVPN supports WireGuard, OpenVPN, and IKEv2 — but requires manual setup for WireGuard. Before choosing, check our VPN protocols guide to understand which protocol suits your needs. If you want a provider on our best VPN list with built-in WireGuard, NordVPN and Surfshark are top picks. ## FAQ **Is PrivateVPN safe to use in 2026?** PrivateVPN uses AES-256 encryption and supports OpenVPN, IKEv2, and WireGuard (via manual setup). The kill switch works reliably on Windows, macOS, and Android. However, PrivateVPN has never undergone an independent security audit, so its no-logs claim remains unverified. It is safe enough for general browsing and streaming but not recommended for high-risk privacy scenarios like whistleblowing or journalist source protection. **Does PrivateVPN work with Netflix?** Yes. As of March 2026, PrivateVPN reliably unblocks Netflix US and approximately 15 other regional Netflix libraries. It also works with Disney+, BBC iPlayer, Amazon Prime Video, Hulu, and HBO Max. Streaming is one of PrivateVPN's strongest features relative to its price. **Is PrivateVPN good for torrenting?** Yes, PrivateVPN is a solid choice for torrenting. All servers allow P2P traffic, the kill switch prevents IP leaks, and port forwarding is enabled by default — a feature most VPNs no longer offer. Port forwarding can significantly improve torrent download speeds by allowing incoming peer connections. The main caveat is the unaudited no-logs policy. **Does PrivateVPN support WireGuard?** PrivateVPN supports WireGuard, but only through manual configuration. You need to download WireGuard configuration files from PrivateVPN's website and import them into the standalone WireGuard client. The WireGuard protocol is not integrated into PrivateVPN's apps, unlike NordVPN (NordLynx), Surfshark, and ExpressVPN (Lightway), which offer one-click fast protocol switching. **How many devices can I connect with PrivateVPN?** PrivateVPN allows up to 10 simultaneous device connections on all plans. You can install the app on unlimited devices, but only 10 can be actively connected at the same time. This matches NordVPN's 10-device limit and is double Mullvad's 5-device limit, though Surfshark still offers unlimited connections. **Is PrivateVPN based in a privacy-friendly jurisdiction?** No. PrivateVPN is headquartered in Sollentuna, Sweden, which is part of the EU and the 14 Eyes intelligence-sharing alliance. While Swedish law does not mandate VPN data retention, the 14 Eyes membership means authorities can share intelligence with partner nations. PrivateVPN argues this is irrelevant because it collects no user data, but this claim has not been independently verified through an audit. ## Sources
